topic.name: Alternative Fuel Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at promoting the use of alternative fuels and reducing reliance on traditional fossil fuels in transportation.

Document 1: Requires each fleet of a manufacturer of passenger automobiles to comprise at least 50% qualified vehicles in model year 2014
Defines 'qualified vehicle' as a vehicle that operates solely on natural gas, hydrogen, or biodiesel.
Document 2: Prohibits the Environmental Protection Agency from establishing or enforcing limits on aggregate carbon dioxide emissions from a state.
Document 4: Amends the Clean Air Act to eliminate the volume standards under the renewable fuel program applicable to corn-starch ethanol.
Document 5: Requires each manufacturer to ensure that at least 50% of model year automobiles and light duty trucks are dual fueled.
Provides for infrastructure installation subgrants to direct retailers of ethanol fuel blends.
Document 7: Amends the Clean Air Act to revise the Renewable Fuel Program to provide that the applicable volume of renewable fuel required in transportation fuel shall apply in the aggregate to renewable fuel and domestic alternative fuel.
Defines 'domestic alternative fuel' as ethanol that is produced from natural gas.
Document 9: Amends the Clean Air Act by revising the renewable fuel program, which requires transportation fuel to contain a minimum volume of renewable fuel.
The Environmental Protection Agency must cap the total volume of ethanol blended into the transportation fuel supply at 9.7% of projected gasoline demand.
Document 10: Amends the Clean Air Act to redefine the term 'cellulosic biomass ethanol' to mean ethanol derived from any cellulose, hemicellulose, or lignin that is derived from renewable biomass.
Increases the volume of renewable fuel that gasoline is required to contain for 2008-2012.

topic.name: Water Recycling and Conservation
topic.description: Legislative initiatives focused on the design, planning, and construction of water recycling and conservation projects across various regions in the United States.

Document 1: authorize the Secretary of the Interior, in cooperation with the Rancho California Water District, to participate in the design, planning, and construction of permanent facilities for water recycling
Limits the federal share to the lesser of 25% of the total project cost or $20 million.
Document 2: authorize the Secretary of the Interior to participate in the design, planning, and construction of recycled water distribution systems in California
Limits the federal share of each project to 25%.
Document 3: authorize the Secretary of the Interior to participate in the design, planning, and construction of projects to treat impaired surface water, reclaim and reuse impaired groundwater
Limits the federal share of each of the project costs to 25%.
Document 6: authorize the Secretary of the Interior to amend an existing contract or enter into one or more new contracts for the storage and conveyance of nonproject water
Makes the costs of constructing, operating, and maintaining any additional infrastructure the responsibility of the non-federal entity.
Document 7: authorize specified additional projects for water conservation and improvement in Cameron, Hidalgo, Willacy, Hudspeth, and El Paso counties, Texas
Permits each project to include the replacement of irrigation canals and lateral canals with buried pipelines.

topic.name: Medicare and Medicaid Legislation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses legislation aimed at modifying Medicare and Medicaid policies to improve access to healthcare services and prescription drugs for beneficiaries.

Document 1: Rural Access to Hospice Act of 2016
amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to allow payment under Medicare for certain services furnished to hospice patients by rural health clinics
Document 2: Medicare Drug Savings Act of 2015
requires drug manufacturers to pay the Secretary of Health and Human Services drug rebates for rebate eligible (low-income) individuals
Document 3: Medicare Drug Savings Act of 2011
requires drug manufacturers to pay the Secretary of Health and Human Services drug rebates for rebate eligible (low-income) individuals
Document 4: Medicaid Advanced Practice Nurses and Physician Assistants Access Act of 2011
amends title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act to eliminate the state option to include nurse practitioners, certified nurse-midwives, and physician assistants as primary care case managers
Document 5: Medicaid Advanced Practice Nurses and Physician Assistants Access Act of 2009
amends title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act to eliminate the state option to include nurse practitioners, certified nurse-midwives, and physician assistants as primary care case managers
Document 6: Medicaid Advanced Practice Nurses and Physician Assistants Access Act of 2007
amends title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act to eliminate the state option to include nurse practitioners, certified nurse-midwives, and physician assistants as primary care case managers
Document 8: Medicare Patient Empowerment Act of 2015
amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to allow any Medicare beneficiary to enter into a contract with an eligible professional
Document 9: Patients' Access to Treatments Act of 2013
amends the Public Health Service Act to establish cost-sharing limits for health plans that cover prescription drugs
Document 10: Patients' Access to Treatments Act of 2012

amends the Public Health Service Act to prohibit a health plan offering group or individual health insurance that provides coverage for prescription drugs from imposing co-payment, coinsurance, or other cost-sharing requirements

topic.name: Employment Discrimination Legislation
topic.description: The documents focus on various legislative acts aimed at protecting employee rights and addressing discrimination in the workplace.

1: Protecting Older Workers Against Discrimination Act
amends the Age Discrimination in Employment Act of 1967
2: Protecting Older Workers Against Discrimination Act
specify that an unlawful employment practice is established when the complaining party demonstrates that age or participation in investigations was a motivating factor
3: declares that it is the policy of the United States that every woman has the fundamental right to choose to bear a child or terminate a pregnancy
4: prohibits a federal, state, or local governmental entity from denying or interfering with a woman's right to exercise such choices
7: amends the National Labor Relations Act to make it an unlawful labor practice for a labor organization to interfere with the rights of employees
9: requires the National Labor Relations Board to certify a bargaining representative without directing an election if a majority of the bargaining unit employees have authorized designation of the representative

topic.name: Social Welfare Legislation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts aimed at improving social welfare programs, including housing assistance, foster care, and support for Native American languages.

Document 1: Repeals the Legal Services Corporation Act
provide financial support for legal assistance
Document 2: Amends the Native American Programs Act of 1974
ensure the survival and continuing vitality of Native American languages
Document 3: study the recruitment and retention of foster parents
foster care program funded under part E
Document 4: provide 150,000 incremental vouchers to public housing agencies
tenant-based rental housing assistance under section 8
Document 5: provide 150,000 incremental vouchers to public housing agencies
tenant-based rental housing assistance under section 8
Document 6: include American Samoa in the Department of Labor's program
public employment services through a one-stop services delivery system
Document 7: provide 150,000 incremental vouchers to public housing agencies
tenant-based rental housing assistance under section 8
Document 8: include the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands in the U.S. Employment Service
public employment services through a one-stop services delivery system
Document 9: eliminate the requirement for foster care maintenance payments
income eligibility standards that treat all children equally
Document 10: eliminate the requirement for foster care maintenance payments
income eligibility standards that treat all children equally

topic.name: Federal Regulatory Reform
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at reforming federal regulatory processes and enhancing accountability in rulemaking.

Document 1: This bill directs the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission (FERC)...
FERC shall extend for up to 3 consecutive 2-year periods the time period during which the licensee must commence project construction.
Document 2: Amends the Congressional Budget and Impoundment Control Act of 1974 to revise the definition of 'agency'...
provisions concerning federal mandates.
Document 3: 10th Amendment Regulatory Reform Act - Authorizes a designated state official to file with the head of a federal agency proposing a rule...
to challenge the constitutionality of the rule under the Tenth Amendment.
Document 4: This bill permits the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission (FERC)...
to extend for up to three consecutive two-year periods from the expiration date of the extension originally issued by FERC.
Document 5: Unfunded Mandates Accountability Act of 2014 - Revises rulemaking requirements under the Unfunded Mandates Reform Act of 1995...
expand judicial review of agency rulemaking.
Document 6: Unfunded Mandates Accountability Act of 2016 - This bill revises rulemaking requirements under the Unfunded Mandates Reform Act of 1995...
expand judicial review of agency rulemaking.
Document 7: Regulations From the Executive in Need of Scrutiny Act of 2015 - States that the purpose of this Act is to increase accountability for and transparency in the federal regulatory process...
require a federal agency promulgating a rule to include in its report to Congress...
Document 8: Restoring the 10th Amendment Act - Authorizes a designated state official to file with the head of a federal agency proposing a rule...
to challenge the constitutionality of the rule under the Tenth Amendment.
Document 9: Unfunded Mandates Accountability Act of 2011 - Amends the Unfunded Mandates Reform Act of 1995 to...
expand provisions relating to judicial review of regulatory impact analyses.
Document 10: Unfunded Mandates Accountability Act of 2013 - Amends the Unfunded Mandates Reform Act of 1995 to...
expand provisions relating to judicial review of regulatory impact analyses.

topic.name: Telecommunications and Privacy Legislation
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative amendments related to telecommunications regulations, privacy protections, and the management of personal information.

Document 1: Directs the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) to revise the do-not-call registry provisions to prohibit politically-oriented recorded message telephone calls.
Defines politically-oriented recorded message telephone call.
Document 2: Amends the Communications Act of 1934 to require each satellite carrier providing secondary transmissions to carry signals of all television broadcast stations located within any local market.
Subject to existing broadcast signal retransmission consent provisions.
Document 3: Prohibits a provider of commercial mobile services from including the wireless telephone number of any subscriber in any wireless directory assistance service database unless certain conditions are met.
Requires a provider to remove the wireless number of any subscriber from a WDAS upon request of that subscriber and without subscriber cost.
Document 4: Excludes from the definition of 'common carrier' a provider of an information service when engaged in providing such service.
Classifies broadband Internet access service as an 'information service' under the Communications Act of 1934.
Document 5: Amends the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act of 1978 to limit the FBI's authority to seek business records pertaining to U.S. persons for foreign intelligence investigations.
Removes the FBI's authority to make such applications for investigations regarding a U.S. person.
Document 6: Makes it unlawful for any person in connection with any telecommunication service to transmit misleading or inaccurate caller identification information with intent to defraud.
Prohibits spoofing.
Document 7: Prohibits data brokers from obtaining or disclosing personal information through fraudulent means.
Defines 'data broker' as a commercial entity that collects personal information to sell or provide third-party access.
Document 8: Prohibits data brokers from obtaining or causing to be disclosed personal information through fraudulent statements.
Requires data brokers to ensure the accuracy of the personal information they maintain.
Document 9: Excludes from the definition of 'common carrier' a provider of an information service when engaged in providing such service.
Defines broadband Internet access service under the Communications Act of 1934.
Document 10: Amends the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act of 1978 to require the FBI to include a statement regarding the relevance of information sought in investigations.
Requires minimization procedures for the retention and dissemination of information.

topic.name: Consumer Credit Regulation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at amending existing laws to protect consumers in credit transactions and mortgage lending.

1: Amends the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 to require an issuer that is a savings and loan holding company to register with the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC)
Requires termination of such registration after a savings and loan holding company certifies that its holders of record of that class of security have been reduced to fewer than 1,200 persons.
2: This bill amends the Truth in Lending Act to create a safe harbor from lawsuit for creditors
A safe harbor from lawsuit is also created for mortgage originators for steering a consumer to a residential mortgage loan.
3: Amends the Truth in Lending Act to make an exemption from the requirement that a creditor establish an escrow or impound account for mandatory periodic payments
Exempts a creditor with less than $1 billion in assets from this requirement.
4: Amends the Truth in Lending Act to prohibit a creditor from extending credit to a consumer under an open end consumer credit plan for which the fee and interest rate exceeds 36%
Sets forth criminal penalties for violation of this Act.
5: Amends the Truth in Lending Act to prohibit a creditor from extending credit to a consumer under an open end consumer credit plan for which the fee and interest rate exceeds 36%
Empowers state Attorneys General to enforce this Act.
6: Amends the Truth in Lending Act with respect to requirements for disclosure to a consumer of points and fees information
Excludes from the computation of such points and fees any compensation paid by a mortgage originator.
7: Amends the Bank Holding Company Act of 1956 to prohibit the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System from determining that real estate brokerage activity is financial in nature
Exempts from such prohibition activities of a bank holding company that directly relate to managing any real property.
8: Amends the Bank Holding Company Act of 1956 to prohibit the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System from determining that real estate brokerage activity is financial in nature
Exempts from such prohibition activities of a bank holding company that directly relate to managing any real property.
9: Amends the Truth in Lending Act with respect to requirements for disclosure to a consumer of points and fees information
Excludes from the computation of such points and fees any compensation paid by a mortgage originator.
10: Amends the Truth in Lending Act with respect to requirements for disclosure to a consumer of points and fees information
Modifies the criteria for exclusion from the computation of points and fees of certain reasonable charges.

topic.name: Regional Development and Innovation
topic.description: The documents focus on initiatives and programs aimed at promoting regional development, transportation planning, and the application of science and technology for economic growth and sustainability.

Document 1: Requires states to cooperate with affected nonmetropolitan local officials responsible for transportation through regional transportation planning organizations
develop and implement long-range statewide transportation plans
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Commerce to establish a competitive grant program to promote domestic regional tourism growth
awards grants to eligible entities to support tourism initiatives
Document 3: Directs the Office of Science and Technology Policy to coordinate international science and technology cooperation
strengthen U.S. science and technology enterprise
Document 4: Establishes the United States Global Development Lab to increase the application of science, technology, innovation and partnerships
discovering, testing, and scaling development innovations
Document 5: Requires the Department of Energy to complete a national plan for smart manufacturing technology development
improve the productivity and energy efficiency of the U.S. manufacturing sector
Document 6: Establish a Global Service Fellowship Program to promote international volunteering opportunities
addressing critical human needs and promoting mutual understanding
Document 9: Directs USAID to develop a strategy to foster sustainable urban development in developing countries
assess the feasibility of establishing a USAID senior advisor for urban sustainable development
Document 10: Establishes the United States Global Development Lab to leverage expertise and resources for program impact
supporting USAID Missions and Bureaus in applying science, technology, innovation, and partnership approaches

topic.name: Agricultural Policy Amendments
topic.description: This cluster discusses various amendments to U.S. agricultural policies and programs, focusing on crop disaster assistance, insurance programs, and regulations affecting farming practices.

1: eliminate the requirement that forage producers must participate in the insurance pilot program
2: consider mung beans and pulse crops as covered commodities
producers on a farm may reduce the base acres for any covered commodity
3: include shellfish as an eligible crop in the noninsured crop disaster assistance program
include shellfish producers in the emergency assistance for livestock program
4: make the current producer payment formula permanent
revise the payment rate adjustment for feed prices
5: provide coverages for eligible crops based on individual yields
reduced premiums for limited resource, beginning, and socially disadvantaged producers
6: permit the taking of any migratory game bird
7: permit the taking of any migratory game bird
8: allow an agricultural producer to take migratory game birds by the aid of baiting
crop was not harvestable due to a natural disaster
9: allow an agricultural producer to take migratory game birds by the aid of baiting
crop was not harvestable due to a natural disaster
10: consider mung beans and pulse crops as covered commodities
producers on a farm may reduce the base acres for any covered commodity

topic.name: Veterans Benefits and Support
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative measures aimed at improving benefits and support for veterans, their families, and caregivers.

Document 1: Amends title II of the Social Security Act with respect to determining entitlement to monthly benefits for caregivers of dependents.
Deems individuals providing care to a dependent relative for up to five years to have been paid a wage.
Document 2: Allows concurrent receipt of military retired pay and veterans' disability compensation for service-connected disabilities.
Makes eligible for full concurrent receipt those separated from military service due to a service-connected disability.
Document 3: Provides extensions on educational assistance under the Montgomery GI Bill for individuals enrolled in educational institutions.
Repeals limits on the use of educational assistance for members of the Selected Reserve.
Document 4: Increases burial and funeral expenses for deceased veterans receiving disability compensation or pension benefits.
Authorizes adjustments to burial allowances based on Social Security Act benefit increases.
Document 5: Repeals provisions requiring offsets of dependency and indemnity compensation from Survivor Benefit Plan annuities.
Restores annuity eligibility to surviving spouses of military personnel.
Document 6: Prohibits terminating TRICARE eligibility for dependents exceeding age limits under certain conditions.
Requires continuation of coverage for eligible dependents.
Document 10: Amends the Family and Medical Leave Act to entitle family members of servicemembers to extended leave for care.
Defines covered servicemember as one undergoing medical treatment for injuries incurred in active duty.

topic.name: Public Health Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving public health through research, prevention, and treatment initiatives for various health conditions.

Document 1: Amends the Public Health Service Act to authorize the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to develop and implement a National Bone Health Program
Requires the Secretary to establish the Osteoporosis and Related Bone Disease Advisory Committee
Document 2: Amends the Public Health Service Act to require the Secretary of Health and Human Services to convene a Research Advisory Committee
Requires the Director of CDC to develop a multisite research project to expand and enhance surveillance data and public health research on gestational diabetes
Document 3: Amends the Public Health Service Act to require the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to make grants to public and private nonprofit entities
Requires the Director of the Center for Mental Health Services to designate a Deputy Director for Older Adult Mental Health Services
Document 4: Amends the Public Health Service Act to require the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to make grants to public and private nonprofit entities
Requires state plans for community mental health services grants to describe the state's outreach to older individuals
Document 5: Amends the Public Health Service Act to require the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to make grants to public and private nonprofit entities
Requires the Director to give special consideration to providing treatment for older adults with substance abuse disorders
Document 6: Amends the Public Health Service Act to direct the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) to develop a multisite gestational diabetes research project
Requires the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) to expand and intensify public health research on gestational diabetes
Document 7: Amends the Public Health Service Act to direct the Director of the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) to develop a multisite gestational diabetes research project
Requires the Director to award grants for demonstration projects to reduce the incidence of gestational diabetes
Document 8: Expresses the sense of Congress that the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) should continue aggressive work on pulmonary hypertension
Requires the Secretary to carry out an educational campaign to increase public awareness of pulmonary hypertension
Document 9: Expresses the sense of Congress that the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) should continue aggressive work on pulmonary hypertension
Requires the Secretary to carry out an educational campaign to increase awareness of pulmonary hypertension among health care providers
Document 10: Amends the Public Health Service Act to require the Secretary of Health and Human Services to award grants for demonstration projects to provide coordinated and integrated services to individuals with mental illnesses
Requires the Secretary to make funds available for the development or expansion of programs to provide integrated treatment services for individuals with a serious mental illness

topic.name: Education Improvement Initiatives
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative acts aimed at improving educational outcomes through targeted support for teachers, principals, and students, particularly in low-performing schools.

Document 8: Directs the Secretary of Education to make grants to states to implement state middle grades needs analyses and improvement plans.
Favors LEAs that serve high proportions of poor children and children attending eligible schools.

topic.name: Federal Recognition of Indian Tribes
topic.description: Legislation extending federal recognition and trust land status to various Indian tribes in the United States, ensuring eligibility for federal services and protections.

Document 1: Extends federal recognition to the following Indian tribes of Virginia
Makes the tribes and their members eligible for all services and benefits provided by the federal government to federally recognized Indian tribes.
Document 2: Extends federal recognition to the following Indian tribes of Virginia
Makes the tribes and their members eligible for all services and benefits provided by the federal government to federally recognized Indian tribes.
Document 3: Places specified federal land within the Park and specified nonfederal land owned by the Quileute Indian Tribe in trust for the Tribe.
Includes those lands in the Quileute Indian Reservation.
Document 4: Places specified federal land within the Park and specified nonfederal land owned by the Quileute Indian Tribe in trust for the Tribe.
Includes those lands in the Quileute Indian Reservation.
Document 7: Requires all interest of the United States in approximately 14,408 acres of land to be held in trust for, and to be part of the reservation of, the Confederated Tribes of Coos, Lower Umpqua, and Siuslaw Indians.
Prohibits gaming on such land.
Document 9: Designates the following areas in California as wilderness areas and as components of the National Wilderness Preservation System.
Releases any parts of the following areas from further study for designation as wilderness.

topic.name: Small Business Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing support and opportunities for small businesses in various capacities.

Document 1: Amends the Small Business Act to increase from 9 to 12 years the authorized period of developmental assistance for participating small businesses.
Increases from 5% to 8% annually the goal of federal procurement contracts let to small businesses owned and controlled by socially and economically disadvantaged individuals.
Document 2: Amends the Small Business Investment Act of 1958 to increase from $225 million to $350 million the maximum amount of outstanding leverage to be made available by the Small Business Administration (SBA).
Document 3: Amends the Small Business Investment Act of 1958 to increase from $225 million to $350 million the maximum amount of outstanding leverage to be made available by the Small Business Administration (SBA).
Document 4: Amends the Small Business Act to increase from $50,000 to $75,000 the maximum loan amount under the Small Business Administration (SBA) microloan program.
Document 5: Authorizes the Administrator of the Small Business Administration (SBA) to make $4 billion in guarantees of debentures of small business investment companies (SBICs).
Amends the Act to increase from $225 million to $350 million the maximum amount of outstanding leverage to be made available by the SBA to two or more commonly-controlled SBICs.
Document 6: Establishes a three-year small business intermediary lending pilot program under which the Administrator of the Small Business Administration (SBA) may make direct loans to eligible nonprofit lending intermediaries.
Authorizes the Administrator, under the program, to make one percent, 20-year loans of up to $3 million to up to 20 lending intermediaries.
Document 7: Amends the Small Business Act to make small businesses owned and controlled by service-disabled veterans eligible for the award by the Small Business Administration (SBA) of small business development grants.
Document 8: Amends the Small Business Act to make small businesses owned and controlled by service-disabled veterans eligible for the award by the Small Business Administration (SBA) of small business development grants.
Document 9: Amends the Small Business Act to authorize the Administrator of the Small Business Administration (SBA) to award contracts to small businesses owned and controlled by service-disabled veterans.
Document 10: Amends the Small Business Act with respect to the HUBZone program to authorize a contracting opportunity to be awarded under the program on the basis of competition restricted to qualified HUBZone small businesses.

topic.name: Immigration Policy and Child Repatriation
topic.description: The documents discuss various acts related to immigration policy, particularly focusing on the treatment and repatriation of unaccompanied alien children and the adjustment of immigration visa categories.

1: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to include within the H-2A nonimmigrant visa category (temporary agricultural workers) an alien coming temporarily to the United States to work as a sheepherder, goat herder, or dairy worker.

Authorizes such alien to petition to become a lawful permanent resident after having worked as a sheepherder, goat herder, or dairy worker in the United States for not fewer than 33 of the preceding 36 months.
2: This bill amends the William Wilberforce Trafficking Victims Protection Reauthorization Act of 2008 to provide that any unaccompanied alien child (UAC) who has not been a victim of a severe form of trafficking in persons... shall be placed in removal proceedings.
The Department of State shall negotiate agreements, in addition to countries contiguous to the United States, regarding the repatriation of children.
3: This bill amends the William Wilberforce Trafficking Victims Protection Reauthorization Act of 2008 to eliminate the special repatriation requirements for unaccompanied alien children (UAC) who are nationals or residents of a country contiguous to the United States.
An immigration officer who finds an inadmissible UAC at a land border or port of entry shall return the child to his or her country of nationality or of last habitual residence.
4: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to direct the Secretary of State, upon notice by the Secretary of Homeland Security (DHS) that a foreign government denies or unreasonably delays accepting the return of an alien... to stop granting immigrant visas or nonimmigrant visas to citizens, subjects, nationals, and residents of that country until the country has accepted the alien.
5: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to direct the Secretary of State, upon notice by the Secretary of Homeland Security (DHS) that a foreign government denies or unreasonably delays accepting the return of an alien... to stop granting immigrant visas or nonimmigrant visas to citizens, subjects, nationals, and residents of that country until the country has accepted the alien.
6: This bill amends the William Wilberforce Trafficking Victims Protection Reauthorization Act of 2008 to eliminate the special repatriation requirements for unaccompanied alien children (UAC) who are nationals or residents of a country contiguous to the United States.
An unaccompanied child who is not a victim of a severe form of trafficking in persons... shall have a hearing before an immigration judge within 14 days.
7: Amends the William Wilberforce Trafficking Victims Protection Reauthorization Act of 2008 to eliminate the special repatriation requirements for unaccompanied alien children (UAC) who are nationals or residents of a country contiguous to the United States.
Requires an immigration officer who finds an inadmissible UAC at a land border or port of entry to return the child to his or her country of nationality or of last habitual residence.
8: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to establish the fiscal year worldwide level of employment-based immigrants at 140,000 plus: the previous year's unused visas; and the number of unused visas from FY1992-FY2007.
Establishes the fiscal year worldwide level of family-sponsored immigrants at 480,000 plus: the previous year's unused visas; and the number of unused visas from FY1992-FY2007.
9: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to establish the fiscal year worldwide level of employment-based immigrants at 140,000 plus: the previous year's unused visas; and the number of unused visas from FY1992-FY2007.
Establishes the fiscal year worldwide level of family-sponsored immigrants at 480,000 plus: the previous year's unused visas; and the number of unused visas from FY1992-FY2007.
10: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to establish the fiscal year worldwide level of employment-based immigrants at 140,000 plus: the previous year's unused visas; and the number of unused visas from FY1992-FY2007.
Establishes the fiscal year worldwide level of family-sponsored immigrants at 480,000 plus: the previous year's unused visas; and the number of unused visas from FY1992-FY2007.

topic.name: Gun Control and Domestic Violence
topic.description: Legislation aimed at addressing gun ownership and restrictions related to domestic violence, stalking, and the welfare of vulnerable populations.

Document 1: Strengthening Investigations of Sex Offenders and Missing Children Act of 2011
investigate cases involving a sex offender who violates a sex offender registration requirement
Document 2: Protecting Domestic Violence and Stalking Victims Act of 2013
Prohibits the sale or other disposition of a firearm to any person knowing or having reasonable cause to believe that such person has been convicted of a misdemeanor crime of stalking
Document 10: Protecting Domestic Violence and Stalking Victims Act of 2015
makes it a crime to knowingly sell or dispose of a firearm to a person who has been convicted of a misdemeanor crime of stalking

topic.name: Federal Funding for Transportation and Local Governments
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various federal acts that authorize funding and grants for transportation projects and local government fiscal assistance.

Document 4: authorizes the Secretary of Transportation to make emergency grants to states for public transportation projects

topic.name: Federal Government Transparency and Reform
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at improving the transparency, efficiency, and effectiveness of federal government operations and programs.

Document 1: Establishes a 16-member Commission on Freedom of Information Act Processing Delays
conduct a study concerning methods to reduce delays in processing Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) requests
Document 2: Establishes the Commission on Government Transformation

Document 4: Requires the Director of the Office of Management and Budget (OMB) to create a separate website about the reports issued by each Office of Inspector General
Document 5: Requires the Director of the Office of Management and Budget (OMB) to create a separate website about the reports issued by each Office of Inspector General
Document 6: Establishes the Federal Agency Program Realignment and Closure Commission

Document 7: Establishes the Federal Agency Program Realignment and Closure Commission
submit to the President and Congress a list of the agencies and programs it recommends eliminating or realigning

Document 10: Establishes the Commission on Government Transformation

topic.name: Acrylic Fiber Tariff Suspension
topic.description: The documents discuss the temporary suspension of duties on various types of acrylic and modacrylic fibers as outlined in the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States.

Document 1: extends the temporary suspension of duty on modacrylic staple fibers
containing 35 percent or more but not over 85 percent by weight of acrylonitrile units
Document 2: extends the temporary suspension of duty on acrylic staple fibers
containing 85 percent or more by weight of acrylonitrile units
Document 3: extends the temporary suspension of duty on modacrylic staple fibers
with an average decitex of 1.9
Document 4: extends the temporary suspension of duty on acrylic staple fibers
containing at least 85 percent by weight of acrylonitrile units
Document 5: suspend temporarily the duty on acrylic filament tow
containing 85 percent or more by weight of acrylonitrile units
Document 6: extends the temporary suspension of duty on acrylic staple fibers
raw white (undyed), crimped
Document 7: extends the temporary suspension of duty on acrylic staple fibers
with an average decitex of 2.2
Document 8: extends the temporary suspension of duty on acrylic filament tow
with an aggregate filament measure in the tow bundle from 660,000 to 1,200,000 decitex
Document 9: extends the temporary suspension of duty on acrylic fiber tow
with an average decitex of 3.3

topic.name: Public Health and Safety Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving public health and safety through regulation of aviation, pharmaceuticals, food safety, and consumer products.

Document 1: installation of secondary barriers to prevent access to the flight deck of each commercial aircraft
Document 2: allows a drug to be imported by a person other than the drug's manufacturer if the drug has the same active ingredients
Document 3: deems a food to be adulterated if its container is composed of bisphenol A
Document 4: requires any household cleaning product to bear a label with a complete and accurate list of all the product's ingredients
Document 5: requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services to deny an application for a new animal drug that is a critical antimicrobial animal drug
Document 6: provides factors to determine whether a controlled substance analogue was intended for human consumption
Document 7: requires the Secretary to withdraw approval of a nontherapeutic use of such drugs in food-producing animals
Document 8: amends provisions governing the importation of prescription drugs
Document 9: prohibits the importation of a qualifying drug unless such drug is imported by a registered importer
Document 10: states that the resale in the United States of prescription drugs that were properly sold abroad is not patent infringement

topic.name: Oil and Gas Leasing Regulations
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative acts and regulations that prohibit or regulate oil and gas exploration and extraction activities in designated areas of the Outer Continental Shelf.

Document 1: Prohibits the Secretary of the Interior from issuing a lease, permit, or license for oil exploration or extraction from specified submerged Outer Continental Shelf lands located within 125 miles of the coast of New Jersey.
including areas with respect to which a moratorium on oil and gas leasing activities existed before enactment of this Act.
Document 6: Amends the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act to prohibit the conduct of oil and gas preleasing, leasing, and related activities in areas of the outer Continental Shelf located in the North Aleutian Basin Planning Area.
Amends the Energy Policy Act of 2005 to exclude such Planning Area from a mandated inventory of oil and natural gas resources beneath the waters of the outer Continental Shelf.
Document 7: Amends the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act to prohibit oil and gas preleasing, leasing, and related activities in areas of the Outer Continental Shelf located off the coast of California.

Amends the Energy Policy Act of 2005 to exclude such areas from the waters of the U.S. Outer Continental Shelf whose oil and natural gas resources the Secretary of Energy is required to inventory and analyze.

topic.name: Student Loan and Election Campaign Reforms
topic.description: This cluster of documents addresses legislative amendments related to student loan regulations and federal election campaign practices.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to permit candidates for election to federal office to designate an individual for campaign committees in the event of the candidate's death.
Prescribes procedures for filing and revoking such a designation.
Document 2: Christopher Bryski Student Loan Protection Act - Amends the Truth in Lending Act to require lenders of private educational loans for which cosigners are held jointly liable to describe clearly and conspicuously, in writing, the cosigners' obligations regarding such loans.
Directs the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB) to publish a model form for describing those obligations.
Document 3: Parent PLUS Loan Improvement Act of 2016 - modifies terms and conditions with respect to Federal Direct PLUS Loans to parent borrowers.
It reduces the interest rate and eliminates the origination fee for a parent PLUS loan disbursed on or after July 1, 2016.
Document 4: Student Loan Fair Prepayment Act - requires a student loan borrower's prepayment amount on various federal loans to be applied in a specific order.
Amends the Truth in Lending Act to require a borrower's prepayment amount on a private education loan to be applied first toward outstanding fees.
Document 5: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to revise the requirement of a 48-hour notification of a campaign contribution of $1,000 or more to the Federal Election Commission (FEC).
Requires Senate candidates to file designations, statements, and reports directly with the FEC.
Document 6: Federal Student Loan Refinancing Act - allows borrowers to consolidate loans as Federal Direct Consolidation Loans.
Sets the interest rate on Federal Direct Consolidation Loans at 4%.
Document 7: Federal Student Loan Refinancing Act - directs the Department of Education to consolidate DLs.
Prohibits any benefit a borrower is receiving from being affected by the consolidation.
Document 8: Real Time Transparency Act of 2015 - amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to revise notification requirements for campaign contributions.
This requirement shall now apply to any political committee regarding cumulative contributions.
Document 9: Real Time Transparency Act of 2015 - revises the requirement of a 48-hour notification of a campaign contribution of $1,000 or more to the FEC.
Any amount transferred by a joint fundraising committee shall be treated as a contribution.
Document 10: Christopher Bryski Student Loan Protection Act - requires lenders to describe cosigners' obligations regarding private education loans.
Amends the Higher Education Act of 1965 to require institutions to provide borrowers information on the effect of death or disability on loans.

topic.name: Tax Code Amendments
topic.description: This cluster discusses various amendments to the Internal Revenue Code aimed at modifying tax treatments for businesses and individuals.

Document 1: amends the Internal Revenue Code
make permanent the increased $500,000 limitation
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code
revises the tax treatment of S corporations
Document 3: Amends Internal Revenue Code provisions relating to real estate investment trusts
treat passive foreign exchange gains as qualifying REIT income
Document 4: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to extend through 2007
the offset of nonrefundable personal tax credits against regular and alternative minimum tax
Document 5: Amends the Internal Revenue Code
allow a qualified small business to elect to use a portion of its tax credit
Document 6: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to revise the tax treatment of S corporations
making permanent the rule requiring a basis adjustment to stock
Document 7: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to revise the tax treatment of S corporations
allowing ESBTs to claim expanded charitable tax deductions
Document 8: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to make permanent the tax credit for increasing research activities
allow an offset of such credit against liability for the alternative minimum tax
Document 9: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to revise the tax treatment of S corporations
allowing individual retirement accounts to be S corporation shareholders
Document 10: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to reduce to 23% the maximum income tax rate for corporations
make permanent the increased ($250,000) expensing allowance for depreciable business assets
